name: Fiji Crested Iguana
conservationEfforts: National Trust of Fiji protects Yadua Taba island (main population). Goat eradication on key islands. Captive breeding at Kula Eco Park.
description: One of the most striking reptiles in the Pacific, with bright emerald-green banding and a tall crest. It was only described by science in 1979. Its island forest habitat is disappearing rapidly.
